data.title: Thailand and U.S. Aim to Conclude Online Tariff Talks by July Deadline
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Thailand and the United States are preparing to finalize their online tariff negotiations as the July 9 deadline set by U.S. President Donald Trump approaches. Chotima Iemsawasdikul, Director-General of Thailand's Trade Negotiations Department, stated during a briefing that Thailand aims to complete the trade discussions before the grace period established by Washington ends. Although a specific date has not been confirmed, preparations for the negotiations with the United States are underway.

data.title: Smarter Web Company Increases Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: According to BlockBeats, British publicly traded company Smarter Web Company has increased its Bitcoin holdings by acquiring an additional 74.27 BTC. Following this acquisition, the company's total Bitcoin reserves have reached 242.34 BTC.

data.title: A fire broke out at Iran's Tabriz airport minutes after the city was hit by a new wave of attacks
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: A fire broke out at Iran's Tabriz Airport. The city was hit by a new round of attacks a few minutes ago. (Jinshi)

data.title: Web3 Investment Company JDI Announces Full Acquisition of Blockchain Project Moonchain
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Web3 investment company JDI announced that it has fully acquired the blockchain project Moonchain. Moonchain was founded by former engineers from Parity Technologies and ZKsync, focusing on blockchain protocols, L2 expansion and DePIN technology. JDI said that this acquisition will combine its hardware manufacturing capabilities with Moonchain's technical foundation to advance its layout in the field of decentralized infrastructure. Moonchain plans to launch the first round of hardware gifts (iHO) at the end of June, and users can obtain smart devices connected to the Web3 network by staking tokens.

data.title: Market Volatility Surges Following Geopolitical Tensions and Tech Disruptions
data.description:
According to PANews, recent geopolitical tensions have led to significant market fluctuations. An Israeli airstrike on Iranian nuclear facilities resulted in the death of Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps Commander Salami, causing a surge in safe-haven assets such as oil and gold. The S&P 500 futures fell below 6000 points, while Bitcoin (BTC) dropped approximately 3% and Ethereum (ETH) declined by 9%. The volatility in the crypto market has increased sharply, with BTC front-end put options premiums rising by five volatility points, indicating strong demand for risk aversion.

Simultaneously, a large-scale internet outage in the United States impacted technology stocks and triggered over $1 billion in long positions liquidations in the crypto market. Despite these challenges, institutional demand continues to support BTC. DeFi Development Corp has announced a $5 billion allocation to Solana (SOL), highlighting sustained confidence in mainstream cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Options Expiry Highlights Market Trends
data.description:
According to PANews, data from Adam@Greeks.live indicates that on June 13, 28,000 Bitcoin (BTC) options and 244,000 Ethereum (ETH) options are set to expire. The BTC options have a put-call ratio of 0.9, with a maximum pain point at $106,000 and a nominal value of $2.93 billion. Meanwhile, ETH options show a put-call ratio of 1.13, with a maximum pain point at $2,650 and a nominal value of $620 million. The delivery volume accounts for approximately 8% of the total open interest, marking a decline following a rebound last week.

In terms of implied volatility (IV), BTC remains at a low level, whereas ETH has seen a noticeable increase, suggesting greater operational space for volatility strategies on ETH. The market volatility risk premium (VRP) is high this week, indicating a state of extremely low market volatility. Despite some adjustments today, the VRP remains elevated.

Additionally, data from block trades show that major market players are increasing their positions in put options, entering a defensive phase. Recent geopolitical factors have heightened market risk aversion, leading to a significant pullback in cryptocurrency prices.

data.title: Iranian government: The attack would not have been possible without US coordination and permission
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Iranian government: Without the coordination and permission of the United States, the attack would not have happened. (Jinshi)

data.title: Israel lowers public security alert level
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Israeli military has recently issued a statement to adjust the public safety defense guidelines for the public. According to the latest requirements, the public no longer needs to stay near the shelters. Previously, the Israeli army required the public to stay near the shelters until further notice. Except for necessary work, educational activities, gatherings and other activities are prohibited. (Jinshi)

data.title: TRON Mainnet TRX Production Reduction Proposal Passed with High Votes: Block Rewards Reduced by 50%, Voting Rewards Reduced by 20%
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News On June 13, TRON announced that the mainnet proposal No. 102 on TRX production reduction had successfully completed voting at 14:00 Singapore time on the same day, and was passed with 25 votes in favor (a total of 27 votes), which reflects the community's strong support for the strategy of accelerating deflation and optimizing the economic model, and also means that TRX production reduction has entered the formal implementation stage.
The implementation of this proposal will reduce the block reward from the original 16 TRX to 8 TRX (a decrease of 50%), and the voting reward from the original 160 TRX to 128 TRX (a decrease of 20%), and it is expected that the annualized deflation rate of TRX will increase from 0.85% to 1.29%. This production reduction aims to accelerate the deflation process of TRX and optimize TRON's economic model, thereby ensuring the long-term sustainable development of the network.

data.title: Viewpoint: The latest conflict between Israel and Iran is just short-term noise, and we should seize the opportunity to buy stocks at a low price
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Economist Alex Krüger published an analysis saying, "The latest confrontation between Israel and Iran should have only a short-term impact, not start a new trend, but just short-term noise. It is more like an Israeli-Iranian armed conflict in 2024, not a large-scale war. The question is when to "buy on dips." Reviewing the 2024 timeline: April 12: Rumors spread, and the market fell for the first time; April 13: Iran launched missiles directly at Israel for the first time, and the market fell again; April 14-18: The market remained nervous as it waited for Israeli retaliation; April 18: Israel retaliated, took restrained attacks, and avoided hitting major targets. The market soared. Both sides then said they would pause and return to covert operations."

data.title: Israel Updates Public Safety Guidelines Amid Security Concerns
data.description: According to Odaily, the Israeli military has issued a new statement revising public safety defense guidelines for citizens. The updated instructions indicate that residents are no longer required to remain near shelters. Previously, the military had advised citizens to stay close to these facilities until further notice. Additionally, activities such as educational programs and gatherings remain prohibited, except for essential work.

In a filing submitted Thursday to the US. District Court for the Southern District of […]
